channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump administration refers NY AG Letitia James for possible prosecution over allegations of mortgage fraud
item.description: New York Attorney General Letitia James faces mortgage fraud allegations stemming from property purchases decades ago in which she allegedly listed her father as her husband.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump signs executive action to lower drug prices
item.description: The action is intended to build upon the existing program for Medicare drug price negotiations, which was created by the Inflation Reduction Act that passed during the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Sydney Lupkin, MPR News

item.title: Trump exempts nearly 70 coal plants from Biden-era rule on mercury and other toxic air pollution
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has granted nearly 70 coal-fired power plants a two-year exemption from federal requirements to reduce emissions of toxic chemicals such as mercury, arsenic and benzene.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Army takes control of federal land along New Mexico border to increase security, protect the environment
item.description: Secretary of the Interior Doug Burgum announced the U.S. Army will take control of federal lands along the U.S.-Mexico border in order to increase security and protect the environment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pentagon senior adviser Dan Caldwell ousted during investigation into leaks
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Dan Caldwell, a senior adviser to Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, has been placed on administrative leave and was escorted out of the Pentagon by security on Tuesday, two defense officials said.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In their words: What judges and Trump's government say about Abrego Garcia's mistaken deportation
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration has dug in on its contention that the government should not have to repatriate Kilmar Abrego Garcia, despite U.S. Supreme Court and lower court rulings that he was wrongly deported and should be returned to the United States. U.S.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: States push Medicaid work rules, but few programs help enrollees find jobs
item.description: Some lawmakers are pushing to require that Medicaid recipients work in order to get or keep coverage, and some states already try to help them find jobs. But the effects of those efforts are unclear.
item.dc:creator: Sam Whitehead, Phil Galewitz, and Katheryn Houghton, MPR News

item.title: AOC claims Republicans will 'trick' Americans into identity politics, stoking racial divisions
item.description: Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez told rally goers Monday evening not to let Republicans "trick" them into thinking they are defined by their race or other immutable characteristics.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NYC Council sues to block Mayor Eric Adams from allowing ICE office on Rikers Island
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City lawmakers are suing to block Mayor Eric Adams from allowing federal immigration agents to operate within the Rikers Island jail complex.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Shapiro arson attack underscores threats to politicians
item.description: As more details emerge, experts said they’ll be watching for responses from top political figures on both sides of the aisle.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Big law spends top dollar as Trump takes aim at firms
item.description: A series of new agreements brokered between President Trump and major law firms suggests a new price tag for those looking to dodge executive orders targeting the legal profession.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: In Pelican Rapids, teens learn the skill of civil discourse
item.description: In a deeply polarized country, a new program in Minnesota is teaching middle and high schoolers how to find common ground in their communities. Program leaders say it’s an effort to inspire young people to become engaged politically and in their towns.
item.dc:creator: Catharine Richert, MPR News

item.title: The Latest: Judge will order sworn testimony by Trump officials in case of wrongly deported man
item.description: A federal judge in Maryland will order sworn testimony by Trump officials to determine if they complied with her orders to facilitate the return of Kilmar Abrego Garcia from an El Salvador prison.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Biden alleges Trump has 'taken a hatchet' to Social Security in his first post-presidency speech
item.description: Former President Joe Biden warns that President Donald Trump has “taken a hatchet” to Social Security, weighing in on a critical issue for millions of Americans.
item.dc:creator: Steve Peoples And Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Deported El Salvador man will stay there: Trump assistant
item.description: Trump special assistant Harrison Fields confirmed the administration will not be attempting to bring Kilmar Abrego Garcia back to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration freezes $2.2 billion in grants to Harvard over campus activism
item.description: The federal government says it’s freezing more than $2.2 billion in grants and $60 million in contracts to Harvard University, since the institution said Monday it won’t comply with the Trump administration’s demands to limit activism on campus.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Former President Joe Biden to speak in Chicago on Tuesday in effort to rally support for Social Security
item.description: Former President Joe Biden is scheduled to speak at the national conference of Advocates, Counselors and Representatives for the Disabled.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, WGN 9

item.title: El Salvador's Bukele says he won’t return Abrego Garcia to US
item.description: President Nayib Bukele called Abrego Garcia “a terrorist” and said he had no power to return Abrego Garcia to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: Lawmakers push to meet with Salvadoran president to plead case of mistakenly deported man
item.description: Lawmakers are pushing to meet with Salvadoran President Nayib Bukele while he’s in Washington amid broader efforts to secure the return of a Maryland man wrongfully deported to a prison in the Central American nation. Sen. Chris Van Hollen (D-Md.) sent a letter to El Salvador’s ambassador to the U.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Minneapolis council member Emily Koski drops out of mayor's race
item.description: Minneapolis City Council member Emily Koski has dropped out of the mayor’s race, according to a campaign update Monday morning.
item.dc:creator: Cari Spencer, MPR News

item.title: Tariffs could impact Tampa Bay shoppers, businesses by summer, economist says
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) — Cheri Hudson owns clothing store Imagine That!!! in South Tampa. It has been her business for 25 years. She is a supporter of President Trump’s trade policies. “I trust Donald Trump. It's only going to be a matter of time. The tariffs are fair.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Beijing slaps 125% tariffs on U.S. goods in latest U.S.-China trade escalation
item.description: China signals the latest tariff hike will be its last round of tit-for-tat measures, prompting sharp falls in European shares, as Asian stocks end the day mixed.
item.dc:creator: Sherisse Pham, MPR News

item.title: Nevada Legislature moving bills as Friday deadline nears; ghost kitchens, open enrollment among winners
item.description: Up against a Friday deadline for bills to be passed out of committee, Nevada lawmakers are working this week to move mountains of legislation in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump threatens to put higher tariff rate back if countries don’t strike deals
item.description: President Trump on Thursday threatened to reverse course on a 90-day pause on country-specific tariffs if countries don’t strike a deal with his administration before then.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5
